text.title: cap-pacelc-walkthrough Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the cap-pacelc-walkthrough methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Apply only to a single microservice-extraction or datastore choice. Never batch unrelated decisions.
rule.rationale: Each split gets its own ADR for traceability.

rule.statement: Every input field has a declared type and source path. Free-form prose without citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Typed + sourced inputs make the output falsifiable.

rule.statement: Output names a single accountable owner. No 'team' / 'we' / empty owner.
Output carries semver version + last_reviewed; stale ADRs are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: ADRs decay; review cadence forced by metadata.

rule.statement: The artefact MUST name CAP mode (AP|CP) AND PACELC mode (EL|EC) — never collapse the two axes into one.
rule.rationale: Conflating CAP and PACELC produces ambiguous decisions; both axes matter.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
